_comment: PoE Heist rogue quick reference. Each rogue has a primary and optionally secondary job skill. Primary = better performance (higher level cap) for that job type.
how_it_works: Each Heist contract requires a specific job type at a minimum skill level. Higher skill level = lower alert generation during the heist. Rogues level up by completing contracts — prioritize levelling your most-used rogues first.

rogues.name: Niles, the Interrogator
rogues.specialty: Best Perception rogue. Valuable for finding hidden items in contracts.
rogues.notes: Run Niles for Div Card reward contracts — his Perception keeps alert low in rooms full of tripwires.

rogues.name: Karst, the Lockpicking Artisan
rogues.specialty: Best Lockpicking rogue. Essential for vault-type contracts.
rogues.notes: The go-to rogue for currency contracts. Level Karst first.

rogues.name: Tibbs, the Giant
rogues.specialty: Only true Brute Force specialist. No secondary job.
rogues.notes: Irreplaceable for Brute Force contracts. His reward type (Uniques) makes him a good choice for unique-hunting runs.

rogues.name: Huck, the Soldier
rogues.specialty: Backup Brute Force rogue with Agility flexibility.
rogues.notes: Good all-rounder when Tibbs is busy or you need Agility coverage.

rogues.name: Isla, the Engineer
rogues.specialty: Best Engineering rogue. Also handles Trap Disarmament as backup.
rogues.notes: Engineering contracts are among the most valuable. Prioritise levelling Isla.

rogues.name: Tullina, the Catburglar
rogues.specialty: Best Agility rogue. High secondary Trap Disarmament makes her extremely versatile.
rogues.notes: Most versatile rogue in the game. Handles Agility and Trap Disarmament contracts well. Level her second after Karst.

rogues.name: Nenet, the Scout
rogues.specialty: Best Counter-Thaumaturgy rogue. Handles magic wards and caster traps.
rogues.notes: The only rogue with strong Counter-Thaumaturgy. Required for high-level Thaumaturgy contracts. Also useful for Map reward contracts.

rogues.name: Vinderi, the Dismantler
rogues.specialty: Best Trap Disarmament rogue. Doubles as Demolition backup.
rogues.notes: Run Vinderi when Tullina is busy. Good for blueprint wing pairs requiring Trap + Demolition.

rogues.name: Gianna, the Master of Disguise
rogues.specialty: Best Deception rogue. Sole Deception specialist — irreplaceable.
rogues.notes: Required for Deception contracts. Deception lowers alert accumulation in guard-heavy areas.

rogues.name: Adiyah, the Wayfinder
rogues.specialty: Best Demolition rogue. Useful for explosive-locked vaults.
rogues.notes: Primary choice for Demolition contracts. Scarab reward type makes her valuable for atlas content.

rogues.name: Vagan, the Bounty Hunter
rogues.specialty: Backup Agility and Brute Force rogue. Less specialised than Tullina or Tibbs.
rogues.notes: Useful for lower-level contracts when primary specialists are on cooldown. Not worth prioritising for levelling.

tips: Level Karst and Tullina first — Lockpicking and Agility are the most common contract types.
Nenet is the only Counter-Thaumaturgy rogue — you cannot skip her for high-tier contracts.
Rogues go on 'cooldown' after each contract run. Rotate rogues so you always have the right specialist available.
Blueprint wings require two rogues simultaneously — plan skill coverage before attempting Grand Heist runs.
Rogue gear (Equipment slot) reduces alert per action. Prioritise upgrading equipment for your most-used rogues.
Completion bonuses are awarded per rogue per unique contract. Run each contract once per rogue for maximum XP.
